page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 48.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25%. The processor supports hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags, which is reflected in its strong Passmark CPU encryption score of 51,326.52. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Passmark physics simulations, also demonstrate strong tier performance. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

The 1:1 resource allocation ratio makes this server highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and the low memory-per-core ratio represent clear tradeoffs, rendering it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching.

This server is best suited for compute-heavy workloads such as multi-threaded batch processing, cryptographic operations, physics simulations, and CPU-based machine learning inference on smaller models.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It delivers 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, paired with 48.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 1.0 GB per core memory allocation. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong performance in both single-core and multi-core CPU execution, particularly in cryptographic tasks and physics simulations, which rank in the top 25%. Conversely, memory bandwidth and web serving throughput sit in the average tier. Given its 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is highly recommended for multi-threaded batch processing, CPU-bound simulations, cryptography, and small-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized virtual machine delivering strong multi-threaded CPU performance and hardware-accelerated cryptography for compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
48.0 GB RAM providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Strong top-25% multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for compute-bound tasks, physics simulations, and batch processing
